label: Iguala Attack Site — Juan N. Álvarez St.
sub: Primary site of police attack on students; multiple students disappeared here

label: Iguala — Periférico Norte Attack
sub: Second attack location; police opened fire on students attempting to flee

label: Iguala — Pueblo Viejo Neighborhood
sub: Third attack location; additional students taken by police and cartel members

label: Cocula Garbage Dump
sub: Government claimed incineration site; debunked by GIEI — fire impossible at scale claimed

label: San Juan River — Barranca La Carnicería
sub: Alleged ash dump site; bone fragment found here later traced to Alexander Mora Venancio; GIEI found evidence of planting

label: Ayotzinapa Normal School
sub: Raúl Isidro Burgos Rural Teachers' College — home institution of the 43 disappeared students

label: Tixtla de Guerrero
sub: Municipality where Ayotzinapa school is located; families gathered here for initial organizing

label: Chilpancingo — State Capital
sub: Guerrero state capital; site of major protests, press conferences, and family meetings with authorities

label: Mexico City — Zócalo Mass Protests
sub: Hundreds of thousands marched from Angel of Independence to Zócalo demanding justice; multiple major marches Oct 2014–Sep 2015

label: Loma de Coyotes
sub: Search site; bone fragments discovered; EAAF conducted forensic analysis

label: Iguala City Hall — Mayor Abarca's Event
sub: Mayor Abarca was hosting a political event when the attacks occurred; his wife María de los Ángeles Pineda Villa was presenting

label: 27th Infantry Battalion Base — Iguala
sub: Mexican Army unit based in Iguala; soldiers monitored events without intervening; central to military complicity allegations

label: Guerreros Unidos Territory — Northern Guerrero
sub: Operating area of Guerreros Unidos cartel; implicated in the disappearances and alleged incineration attempt

label: PGR/FGR Federal Offices — Mexico City
sub: Attorney General's Office; site of 'historical truth' press conference Nov 7, 2014; later arrested Murillo Karam (2022)

label: Autopista Iguala–Chilpancingo Corridor
sub: Route taken by students; multiple roadblocks and interception points by police along this highway
